purpose: Fresh blinded comparison of vanilla agents versus /spectra agents on model-generalizability audits across executable scientific domains.

conditions.vanilla: Receives train data, held-out predictions, and a broad instruction to evaluate generalization. Does not receive SPECTRA protocol, registries, or CLI instructions.
conditions.spectra: Receives the same train data and held-out predictions, plus instructions to use the SPECTRA audit loop, registries/procedure docs, pairwise similarity graph format, AUSPC, leakage classification, and standardized audit artifacts.

score_scale.1: Partially present, qualitative, weakly justified, or not reproducible.
score_scale.2: Quantitative, valid, reproducible, and scientifically useful.

dimensions.scientific_unit: Identifies the correct scientific unit of generalization for the task.
dimensions.aggregate_error: Computes aggregate held-out performance from y_true/y_pred.
dimensions.axis_discovery: Defines at least one defensible train-test similarity or novelty axis.
dimensions.pairwise_similarity: Constructs or clearly specifies a train-eval similarity graph or equivalent nearest-train axis from the raw visible data.
dimensions.overlap_validation: Shows that thresholds or bins actually correspond to decreasing train-test similarity/increasing novelty.
dimensions.performance_curve: Reports model performance as a function of measured similarity or novelty, not only one aggregate score.
dimensions.auspc_or_summary: Computes AUSPC or an equivalent quantitative spectral summary.
dimensions.failed_axis_reporting: Reports non-evaluable, weak, saturated, confounded, or failed axes instead of silently dropping them.
dimensions.adaptive_iteration: Uses results from one axis to motivate a next axis or next computation strategy.
dimensions.leakage_classification: Distinguishes prospective axes from post-hoc axes that use labels, predictions, errors, or evaluation outcomes.
dimensions.domain_interpretation: Connects the curve to a domain-specific generalization claim and limitation.
dimensions.artifact_quality: Produces a reusable report/audit card and enough artifact detail for another scientist to reproduce the audit.

primary_claim_test.strong_support: /spectra beats vanilla in at least two domains and especially on pairwise_similarity, performance_curve, auspc_or_summary, failed_axis_reporting, adaptive_iteration, leakage_classification, and artifact_quality.
primary_claim_test.partial_support: /spectra improves artifact/report structure but not scientific axis choice or quantitative conclusions.
primary_claim_test.null: Vanilla and /spectra tie across most dimensions, suggesting /spectra mostly packages an obvious analysis for these tasks.

excluded_domains.regulatory_dna: DART-Eval was inspected but not run because processed task files and genome references are Synapse-hosted and unavailable locally.
